name: Reduce Ultra-Processed Food
desc: Cap sugar and unhealthy fat intake at planetary health diet levels. Food processing jobs are lost.

name: Less Pork Poultry & Eggs
desc: Cap pig meat poultry and egg intake at planetary health diet levels.

name: Less Beef & Dairy
desc: Cap ruminant meat and dairy at planetary health diet recommendations.

name: More Vegetables & Fruits
desc: Increase vegetable fruit and nut consumption to PHD levels.

name: Reduce Overweight by 50%
desc: Reduce calorie intake to halve overweight and obesity rates. Healthier diets can raise food costs.

name: End Undernutrition
desc: Increase calorie access to eradicate underweight globally by 2050.

name: Halve Food Waste
desc: Cut household and retail food waste to max 20% of intake. Lower demand reduces farm employment.

name: Liberalize Trade
desc: Shift trade toward cost-competitiveness but can entrench large agribusiness.

name: Global Minimum Wage
desc: Raise agricultural wages to at least $3/hour in lower-income countries.

name: Labor-Capital Rebalancing
desc: Substitute capital with labour in overly mechanized agricultural systems.

name: Forest Carbon Pricing (REDD+)
desc: Carbon price on deforestation to protect and regenerate forests.

name: 30x30 Land Conservation
desc: Protect 30% of global land area by 2030 — biodiversity hotspots first.

name: Rewet Peatlands
desc: Incentivize rewetting of drained peatlands via GHG pricing. Productive farmland is retired
poverty: carbon credit markets entrench large landowners.

name: Environmental Water Flows
desc: Legally maintain minimum environmental water flows for ecosystems.

name: Biodiversity Offsetting
desc: Prevent BII decline in any biome — losses must be compensated elsewhere.

name: Nitrogen Use Efficiency
desc: Improve manure application nitrification inhibitors and precision farming.

name: Diverse Crop Rotations
desc: Incentivize longer more diverse crop rotations. Transition costs and yield variability raise food prices.

name: Landscape Habitats
desc: Reserve at least 20% of cropland area for semi-natural habitats. Reduced production area raises food prices.

name: Rice Emission Mitigation
desc: Alternate flooding direct seeding and improved residue management in rice. Less labour-intensive practices displace workers.

name: Livestock Productivity
desc: Improve feed efficiency in ruminant systems in low-income countries. Industrial livestock producers consolidate market power.

name: Manure Management
desc: Anaerobic digesters for 50% of confined livestock manure. Infrastructure costs are passed on through food prices.

name: Soil Carbon Sequestration
desc: Carbon pricing to incentivize soil carbon storage. Carbon markets benefit large landowners and consolidate agribusiness power.

name: Sustainable Demographic Transition
desc: Education and development to slow population growth to 8.9 bn by 2050. Demographic transition shrinks rural labour supply.

name: Equitable Human Development
desc: Faster more equitable growth

name: Energy Transition
desc: Transform energy industry and transport to stay within 1.5°C budget. Higher energy costs raise food production and transport costs.

name: Bioplastics
desc: Use agricultural feedstocks to replace fossil-based plastics and packaging. Biorefinery crops require extra nitrogen inputs and new agro-industrial interests emerge.

name: Timber Cities
desc: Mass timber construction sequesters carbon in buildings replacing concrete and steel. Rapid expansion of forestry plantations threatens natural habitats and benefits large timber interests.

name: Diet Package Synergy
desc: Co-benefits of a comprehensive diet shift: interacting demand changes reinforce each other beyond individual measures.

name: Biosphere Package Synergy
desc: Nature fully protected: landscape connectivity and reduced fragmentation amplify biodiversity gains.

name: Agriculture Package Synergy
desc: Farming in ecological balance: combined precision farming and habitat measures cut nitrogen and water pressure.

name: Cross-Sector Synergy
desc: System-wide transformation: energy and demographic transitions reduce food-system emissions and industry capture.

name: Full Integration Synergy
desc: All packages combined: co-benefits amplify across demand supply biosphere and governance dimensions.
